file.content:
function _clrc_function --description "Run Claude Code remote-control with a stable binary, immune to bun updates while running"
  # Resolve the symlink to the real inode before starting.
  # When bun replaces the file (creates a new inode), the running node process
  # keeps its reference to the old inode and is unaffected.
  # Usage: clrc [<claude remote-control args...>]

  set -l claude_real (realpath (which claude))
  node $claude_real remote-control $argv
end

content.content:
function _clwxe_function --description "Run Claude Code with a free-form prompt while skipping permissions with git worktree integration"
  # Run Claude Code with a free-form prompt (spaces allowed) and bypass permission checks
  # Usage: clwxe [<prompt words...>]

  if test (count $argv) -eq 0
    claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --worktree
  else
    set -l prompt (string join " " -- $argv)
    claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --worktree --print -- "$prompt"
  end
end

toolUseResult.stdout:
function _clwxe_function --description "Run Claude Code with a free-form prompt while skipping permissions with git worktree integration"
  # Run Claude Code with a free-form prompt (spaces allowed) and bypass permission checks
  # Usage: clwxe [<prompt words...>]

  if test (count $argv) -eq 0
    claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --worktree
  else
    set -l prompt (string join " " -- $argv)
    claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --worktree --print -- "$prompt"
  end
end

content.text: Now I understand the patterns. `clwrc` should run remote-control with `--worktree`. Let me create the function and update the config.

input.content:
function _clwrc_function --description "Run Claude Code remote-control with a stable binary in workspace git worktree"
  # Resolve the symlink to the real inode before starting.
  # When bun replaces the file (creates a new inode), the running node process
  # keeps its reference to the old inode and is unaffected.
  # Usage: clwrc [<claude remote-control args...>]

  set -l claude_real (realpath (which claude))
  node $claude_real remote-control --worktree $argv
end

toolUseResult.content:
function _clwrc_function --description "Run Claude Code remote-control with a stable binary in workspace git worktree"
  # Resolve the symlink to the real inode before starting.
  # When bun replaces the file (creates a new inode), the running node process
  # keeps its reference to the old inode and is unaffected.
  # Usage: clwrc [<claude remote-control args...>]

  set -l claude_real (realpath (which claude))
  node $claude_real remote-control --worktree $argv
end

content.text:
Done. Here's a summary of what was added:

- **`clrc`** abbreviation → `_clrc_function` (existing function, now wired in)
- **`clwrc`** abbreviation → `_clwrc_function` (new function)
- **`_clwrc_function.fish`** — runs `claude remote-control --worktree` with the stable realpath binary trick

Both `_clrc_function` and `_clwrc_function` are now in the function file list so home-manager will deploy them.

 # Handle Stop hook
-if echo "$input" | jq -e '.session_id' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+if echo "$input" | jq -e '.hook_event_name == "Stop"' >/dev/null 2>&1; then

input.command:
gh pr create --title "feat(fish): add clrc and clwrc abbreviations for remote-control" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- Add `clrc` abbreviation → `_clrc_function` (stable claude remote-control)
- Add `clwrc` abbreviation → `_clwrc_function` (remote-control in workspace git worktree)
- Register both functions in the fish config function file list

## Test plan
- [ ] `clrc` runs `claude remote-control` with stable realpath binary
- [ ] `clwrc` runs `claude remote-control --worktree` with stable realpath binary
- [ ] Home-manager switch deploys both function files

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"
